item.title: It's noticeable...
item.description:
Heck I want to say probably even further back than 10 years but I don't have have any pictures or ID's from before like 2015... But before I looked like generic blob-ish thug #3 in a mugshot competition. AAAAAAAAAAAAND I just realized I have a sort of "progress" pic now...
Look, I HAVE CHEEKBONES!!!!! and my lips aren't trying to escape into my body... Yes those are weird things to focus on, SHUT UP! I wasn't even TRYING to smile in that last one... Left to right, 2015(look at the size of that deformed melon, dear god) , 2021 (Сволочь?), Now. And to think I was even BIGGER in 2005 (My worst weight) Anyways enough gushing, Still dealing with some health crap but I'm going to try and gear up for a massive first day of Pride Meme dump, expect all the gay(well Bi but whatevs) I can muster and possibly more cuz this has me in a good mood... ^_^

item.title: Little stowaway wants to go to work with mom
item.dc:creator: tampacl

item.title: Pippin slew a troll-chief
item.description:
This is a lore account on the great feat of arms by the Took at the gate to the Land of Shadow.
The hobbit Peregrin 'Pippin' Took was in the Host of the West that marched on the Black Gate, at the northwest of Mordor, in year 3019 of the Third Age. The less than six thousand men attempted to attract the attention of the Dark Lord, Sauron, so that the Ring-bearer had a chance to reach Mordor's volcano where the Master Ring of Sauron could be undone.
The Host of the West consisted mainly of soldiers from Gondor and Rohan. Among the former were Dúnedain citadel guards, that Pippin had joined when he served Steward Denethor, from the capital city Minas Tirith. Among them was Pippin's friend Beregond (art by Tom Romain). There were also the swan-knights serving Prince Imrahil of Dol Amroth (Tolkien used prince in its original sense of high noble, like a duke); in Gondor was Imrahil the greatest nobleman after the Steward.
The Host of the West had taken defensive positions on hills behind mires outside the Black Gate, where they might long break the waves of the forces of Sauron. Hopeless Pippin "stood beside Beregond in the front rank of Gondor with Imrahil’s men. For it seemed best to him to die soon and leave the bitter story of his life, since all was in ruin" (The Return of the King; V; X onwards, art by Kev Crossley).
He wished his friend and fellow hobbit Merry was with him, but Merry had been wounded when the Witch-king was slain and rested still in Minas Tirith. Pippin saw the enemy come charging to the assault (art by Ted Nasmith): "‘Well, well, now at any rate I understand poor Denethor a little better. We might die together, Merry and I, and since die we must, why not? Well, as he is not here, I hope he’ll find an easier end. But now I must do my best.’"
Pippin had as a sword a long enchanted dagger that could break spells (RotK; V; VI), found in a burial mound in the Barrow Downs east of the Shire. It was presumably wrought over 1500 years earlier in Arnor, the northern kingdom of the Dúnedain that later was ruined. The leaf-shaped dagger had "serpent-forms in red and gold. They gleamed as he drew them from their black sheaths, wrought of some strange metal, light and strong, and set with many fiery stones" (The Fellowship of the Ring; I; VIII, art by Alexander Mokhov).
He drew his sword and looked at its intertwining shapes of red and gold; and the flowing characters of Númenor glinted like fire upon the blade (art by incoded): "‘This was made for just such an hour,’ he thought. ‘If only I could smite that foul Messenger with it, then almost I should draw level with old Merry. Well, I’ll smite some of this beastly brood before the end. I wish I could see cool sunlight and green grass again!’"
"Then even as he thought these things the first assault crashed into them. The orcs hindered by the mires that lay before the hills halted and poured their arrows into the defending ranks. But through them there came striding up, roaring like beasts, a great company of hill-trolls out of Gorgoroth." Gorgoroth was the north-western region of Mordor (art by EricVonRuin).
"Taller and broader than Men they were, and they were clad only in close-fitting mesh of horny scales, or maybe that was their hideous hide; but they bore round bucklers huge and black and wielded heavy hammers in their knotted hands. Reckless they sprang into the pools and waded across, bellowing as they came." Trolls were at least 12 feet [3.66 meters] and possibly more in height (art by jdmoorethefirst).
"Like a storm they broke upon the line of the men of Gondor, and beat upon helm and head, and arm and shield as smiths hewing the hot bending iron. At Pippin’s side Beregond was stunned and overborne, and he fell; and the great troll-chief that smote him down bent over him, reaching out a clutching claw; for these fell creatures would bite the throats of those that they threw down" (art by Dmitry Prosvirnin).
"Then Pippin stabbed upwards, and the written blade of Westernesse pierced through the hide and went deep into the vitals of the troll, and his black blood came gushing out. He toppled forward and came crashing down like a falling rock, burying those beneath him. Blackness and stench and crushing pain came upon Pippin, and his mind fell away into a great darkness" (art by Merlkir).
"‘So it ends as I guessed it would,’ his thought said, even as it fluttered away; and it laughed a little within him ere it fled, almost gay it seemed to be casting off at last all doubt and care and fear." He heard men call out that the Eagles were coming and for a moment thought of Bilbo, ere he realised that it was from his tale long, long ago. As for his own, it was at an end and he wished the world goodbye as his eyes saw no more (art by Murr).
But the demise of Sauron, Mordor and the Black Gate came in that hour, when the decoy proved successful and the One Ring was destroyed. The forces of the Dark Lord for the most part fled in panic and the remaining Host of the West survived. Also Pippin lived to tell the tale, he was healed and not long after reunited with his friends Merry, Frodo and Samwise (art by Aegeri).
Note: in the film adaptation by Peter Jackson et al Aragorn fights a troll instead, though this was not intended to take glory from Pippin. Instead the plan was for Aragorn to fight Sauron at the Gate, but once they thankfully realised how at odds with the book that would been they replaced Sauron with a troll.
Some related accounts: Trolls https://imgur.com/gallery/mrTmJa4 The Black Gate https://imgur.com/gallery/P0PvVad Imrahil and the swan-knights https://imgur.com/gallery/uk0749w Merry's blade and the Witch-king's fall https://imgur.com/gallery/GFrn22h — — — — — Index: character bios https://imgur.com/gallery/axV2mPF Index: folks, animals, items, etc https://imgur.com/gallery/AQK9pJL
item.dc:creator: Ngugi

item.dc:creator: LosPer

item.title: Can I give up please?
item.description: It finally happened. After my doctor cleared me to go back to work 3 days a week 1550-1800 hours. That was my schedule. I usually did it 5 days a week but. It took me pleading with her to let me go back 3 days a week. Well I sent in the doctors info to work and the MRI findings from May 22, 2025. My manager called me at 1424 and explained that he felt my safety was most important. The job is not safe for me anymore due to the drivers; possibility of an incident because of a careless driver. If I have a seizure at work it's not gunna be good. My back seems to be the trigger to my seizures and my job requires a bit of up and down over a curb. This is not a good combo and my boss realizes that. The plus side is he wrote me an amazing letter of recommendation and told me that he would gladly accept phone calls about me. But alas I am now unemployed and scared. I have been applying for jobs this entire time and I have been going to interviews. People do not like potential employees who come in with a cane.. I have been trying my ass off to find a job close to home since I use the Portland TriMet system. It's been a nightmare to find a job but I'm trying. I filed for unemployment today as well but I'm not sure if I will get it. Edit: I reached out to a lawyers office. Sent them the information about what happened, per y'all's recommendations. Also please it is She/her. 34 MtF :3 please and thanks lol. Edit 2: This has blown up so I'll add on. Yes I realize now it's stupid I did it. I was bawling my eyes out, in pain. Not sure what to do and being told I can't safely do the job. I have been on medical leave since January 3rd 2025 due to my back issues and concussion. And the seizures started after the concussion. I talked to a lovely lady at the Oregon one benefits center who is also disabled and she gave me a ton of information on what to do. Much like what y'all have told me already. So I have done what is said. I reached out to an employment attorney and explained the case. Right now I can't do much more than wait. For unemployment, I was told I would get it because of the circumstances as long as it is thoroughly explained. I will also talk to my disability lawyer to see if they think anything can/should be done. Or if they have an attorney to tackle this new case
item.dc:creator: LustyLombax

item.title: So many symptoms, so many illnesses, so many diseases, so much, just can't be seen!! Just because you can't see it doesn't mean it doesn't exist! Just be kind and mindful, choose kindness!

item.title: Mermay day 27, elderberry [OC]
item.description:
Here she is!! But careful, elderberries are poisonous when eaten raw- You need to cook them if you plan on having a good time, or better yet, make some elderberry wine! However if you really can’t wait, you can make a delicious cordial from the elderflowers too ;D that’s what I did last week and holy mackerel is it wonderful <3
Only four more days to go?! If you like my stuff and want to see more, come support me on Patreon! —> https://www.patreon.com/AmyRevives
item.dc:creator: AmyRevives

item.title: Cancer Advice is Welcome plus void screaming
item.description: Today was my wife's first day of chemo. She has stage 3 and we have every reason to think she will be able to recover fully, but we have a year of treatment ahead of us, including chemo, surgery, radiation, and hormone therapy. It's a lot! If anyone has suggestions for a spouse supporting a cancer person, please share them! Specifically, things I can do (or say) (or not say) to make this easier on her, and to make sure she feels loved and supported. Or just practical things that might not occur naturally to a halfwit like me. Some thoughts for the Void: -I would not say that she is "strong" or "brave". She just sees what needs to be done and does it. She is matter-of-fact. She has a list of steps to beat cancer and she is taking the steps. I love her so much. -It is my job to run the whole show around her so that she can focus her efforts on beating this. For me, this feels like a major test of my quality as a human being. It is the most important thing I have ever done, and also the most frightening. This is a test I can not fail, but what if I don't measure up? Even thinking about this feels like a failure, because I'm making it about me instead of about her. -When you go on a first date with someone, you never think that someday you'll be going to chemo with that person. Our first date was 30 years ago, when we were 17. Of course we didn't think something like that when we were kids. Nevertheless, this is something I thought about today. -There were a lot of other husbands there with their wives today. One guy in particular I really wished I could talk to. Even though his wife is the sick one, she seemed so much stronger than him physically, but he was shuffling along carrying their little backpack of chemo stuff nonetheless. I wanted to say to him, "You and I are brothers." But everyone there is in their own little world of pain and worry and illness, and I didn't feel confident breaking into his little world. Plus, it is generally frowned on to speak to strangers while they're at a urinal. -My Dad passed in November, and I have been helping my Mom as much as possible since then with whatever I can, but from a distance since we live in different states. Seeing the people who were there at the hospital alone, and the people who were there with family members, it made me very scared that Mom will need me where she is at the same time my wife needs me here. That's all. This post is mostly about me and the things I'm thinking about. Just wanted to park a few things that are on my mind in this process. In our real lives I am not as self-centered and I am focused on my wife. This post is just shouting into the void, as we say. Suggestions welcome.
item.dc:creator: allmylifeleduptothisonepost

item.title: A dump on the theme of Alcohol!
